methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $1.0B - $23.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

private.name: Aeva Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Credo Semiconductor, a public chip and semiconductor company that completed its IPO in 2022, is evaluated against a universe of 11 same-sector peers spanning both public and private stages. The peer set includes pre-IPO players like Cerebras at $23.0B, late-stage private companies such as PsiQuantum at $7.0B and SambaNova Systems at $5.0B, and earlier-stage entrants including Series A Etched and Series D Tenstorrent at $2.7B. Public semiconductor comps (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trade at a median P/S of 24.2x.

The peer-median method is applicable and yields a primary valuation anchor. Across the 11-company comp set, the median valuation stands at $2.3B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $23.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue for the target. Consequently, the triangulation relies on a single methodology.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $2.0B to $2.7B, centered on the peer median of $2.3B. This sits materially below Credo's stated $5B+ current valuation, suggesting either a significant valuation premium relative to the broader peer cohort or material revenue/profitability outperformance not captured in static peer multiples. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and margin profile relative to peers, and whether the current trading valuation reflects post-IPO market repricing versus fundamentals.
